meta.review_note: Chemistry paper: scrutinized as a Lewis-acid/IL cationic-polymerization yield + HRMS-mechanism study (not stats). Deterministic verdicts preserved verbatim (DESIGN §3.1): the c7 mass-balance PASS is untouched and no deterministic verdict was overridden. Recompute-before-advisory (DESIGN honesty): mol%% ratios (c9=58x~60), Table-5 dispersities (c25=4.665~4.67, c26=2.068~2.07), the HRMS aggregate ion (c18 calc 558.1638 vs 558.1672, ~6 ppm) and the two-Cl isotope envelope (c19) were all recomputed and found clean -> CONFIRMED-CORRECT, bare abstain dropped with an auditable claims-auditor note. Two genuine T2 abstract/text-vs-table inconsistencies surfaced by recompute (c4 Tg floor 40 vs table 25 C; c21 Mw floor 1300 vs table 731) are ROUTED not hard-flagged, because the Table-5 bounds are 0.75-confidence aggregate extractions, not per-row data. T4 causal over-statement on c10 (threshold, not monotone trend) and T5 cited-literature values (c5,c6,c12,c13,c22, need source retrieval) routed advisory. Subjective novelty/significance (c1,c18) routed, never scored. Bare abstains eliminated: every claim adjudicated, advised, routed, or reviewed-clean.

findings.message: output mass does not exceed input mass (mass conserved)

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed across skeptic/domain-expert/methodologist/claims-auditor/integrity perspectives: no over-reach found. T2 abstract scope-claim: Table 2 gives 97-99% conversion across 50-90 C (6% only at 40 C, outside the stated range) and Table 3 gives 99% at 0.1 mol% InCl3 -> 'quantitative conversions, 50-90 C, small Lewis-acid loading' is faithful to the data. No numeric verifier covers it yet (synthesis candidate, DESIGN §8); review clears it of claim-vs-evidence over-statement. Numeric recompute deferred to a synthesized T0/T2 verifier.
routed_to_human.quote:
These catalytic systems were effective to polymerize styrene in the temperature range of 50−90 °C, obtaining
quantitative conversions in the presence of ILs in polymerization
catalyzed by small amounts of Lewis acid.

T4 causal/trend over-statement. The claim 'conversion increased with synthesis temperature' implies a monotone trend, but the supporting Table 2 is NOT monotone: conversion is 6% at 40 C then 99,97,98,98,99% at 50-90 C — i.e. a sharp THRESHOLD near 50 C, after which conversion is flat (~98%, even dipping 99->97 at 60 C), not a graded increase. The data support 'above ~50 C the system reaches near-quantitative conversion', not 'conversion increases with temperature'. Confirms-it: a monotonicity/regression check on the (T, conversion) pairs, or rephrasing to a threshold rather than a trend.
routed_to_human.quote:
At temperatures above 50 °C, the system becomes
so reactive that it leads to polystyrene formation in nearly
quantitative yields.

routed_to_human.note: T2 minor: '~99% at 50 C' matches Table 2 (99% at 50 C) exactly; the over-reach is only in the causal framing (see methodologist note), not in the 50 C number itself.

routed_to_human.note: T2 internal-consistency: abstract states Tg '40-71 C', but the extracted Table 5 bounds give Tg_min = 25.0 C (and Tg_max = 71.8 C ~ 71). The upper bound is faithful, but the abstract FLOOR of 40 C appears to exclude a ~25 C sample present in Table 5 — a 15 C gap, larger than rounding. This may be a deliberate scope restriction (e.g. excluding a low-Mn outlier) or an abstract-vs-table inconsistency. Confirms-it: a per-row Tg readback from Table 5 to a T2 prose-vs-table verifier; if the table truly contains a 25 C Tg, the abstract range '40-71' is too narrow on the low end. (Extracted Table 5 bounds carry 0.75 confidence, so this is routed, not hard-flagged.)

routed_to_human.note: T2 internal-consistency: text states Mw range '426,000 to 1300 g/mol'; Table 5 extracted bounds give Mw_min = 731 (and Mw_max = 426,155 ~ 426,000). The upper bound matches, but the stated LOW Mw of 1300 is ~1.78x the table minimum of 731 — not a rounding gap. Either the lowest-Mw sample was excluded from the prose range or this is an abstract/text-vs-table mismatch. Mn range '200,000-600' in the same sentence is consistent (Mn_min=600 exact). Confirms-it: a per-row Mw readback from Table 5 to a T2 verifier; if a 731 g/mol Mw exists, the prose floor 1300 is inconsistent. (Routed not flagged: ev_t5 aggregate bounds are 0.75-confidence extractions.)

routed_to_human.note: Whether mechanistically resolving the role of Lewis-acidic ILs in styrene cationic polymerization via ESI-MS (chloronium/aggregate-ion detection) is a NOVEL/important contribution vs incremental over the cited IL-catalyzed polymerization literature is a value judgement LITMUS does not score (DESIGN §3.5). Routed to an editor/domain reviewer.

routed_to_human.note: Whether the ESI-MS mechanistic evidence (aggregate ion at m/z 558.1672 + chloronium at m/z 313) is SIGNIFICANT enough to establish the proposed cationic mechanism, vs merely consistent with it, is an importance judgement routed to a human (DESIGN §3.5), not scored.
